prose: Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (chemkit tier 'standard'), via PySCF RKS with exact four-centre integrals (no density fitting), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states were treated as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2). Partial charges were obtained with the Mulliken scheme in PySCF. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file with no relaxation at this level (single-point Fukui). The integrity check passed: status=ok, trustworthy=true, sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (most electrophilic site), found at atom 4 (the ipso carbon attached to –NO₂): f⁺ = 0.49641782. The most nucleophilic site is atom 5 (ortho carbon): f⁻ = 0.10728820. The dual descriptor at the ipso carbon is +0.51071 (strongly electrophilic). The two nitro oxygens (atoms 1–2) also show large f⁺ ≈ 0.47. The nitrogen (atom 3) has a large negative dual descriptor (−0.47394), consistent with nucleophilic character. No warnings were raised beyond the routine normalisation confirmation messages (both passing). Command: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui [input.xyz] --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --accept-defaults. Output: nitrobenzene_fukui_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json.
